content: We can draw an unexpected parallel between the linguistic double meaning of reflexion (as both literal optical reflection and deliberate cognitive reflection in high school English curricula) and LLM multi-perspective self-reflection mechanisms: LLM self-reflection literally works like a 'linguistic mirror' that mirrors both its own initial output and alternative inconsistent solving perspectives, just as the word 'reflexion' mirrors two distinct conceptual meanings (physical reflection and metacognition) in the same lexical frame. This framework suggests that lexical semantic ambiguity around 'reflexion' actually prefigured the core functional design of modern LLM self-reflection systems.
